name: Abhinav Avasarala
headline: I’m a full-stack systems engineer who enjoys building software from the ground up, understanding how hardware works, how systems scale, and how to turn that knowledge into fast, reliable applications.
about: My experience spans low-level programming (C/C++/CUDA), backend systems (Python, Node.js), cloud infrastructure (GCP/AWS/Kubernetes), and ML frameworks. I love diving deep into performance, debugging bottlenecks.

bullets: Courses: Software Development Fundamentals; Data Structures & Algorithms; C & Software Tools; Statistics for Engineers; Multivariable Calculus; Operating Systems; Signals & Circuits; Machine Learning; Fundamentals of Logic Design

Supporting enterprise-scale migration of workload scheduling from IBM LSF to Slurm across the Hazel HPC cluster (1,000+ nodes), analyzing job submission patterns and translating batch scripts for production rollout
Built Apptainer container images for GPU-accelerated PyTorch workloads, packaging CUDA/cuDNN dependencies for reproducible, rootless execution across heterogeneous compute nodes

Characterized multi-GPU interconnect topology on GPU nodes, identifying cross-NUMA GPU placement and hybrid InfiniBand/RoCE network paths, and analyzed the resulting bandwidth implications for NCCL collective operations
Automated strong-scaling benchmarks and MPI rank-distribution studies with Python and Slurm job arrays, producing throughput reports that validate cluster performance across node configurations

deepDive.title: Migrating a 1,000+ Node HPC Cluster from LSF to Slurm
deepDive.summary: Supporting an enterprise-scale migration of workload scheduling from IBM LSF to Slurm across NCSU's Hazel HPC cluster, while characterizing GPU interconnect topology and automating scaling benchmarks.
body: Analyzed existing job submission patterns on the 1,000+ node Hazel cluster and translated production LSF batch scripts to Slurm equivalents to support a live scheduler migration.
Packaged GPU-accelerated PyTorch workloads into Apptainer container images, bundling CUDA/cuDNN dependencies for reproducible, rootless execution across heterogeneous compute nodes.
Mapped multi-GPU interconnect topology on GPU nodes — cross-NUMA GPU placement and hybrid InfiniBand/RoCE paths — and analyzed the bandwidth implications for NCCL collective operations.
Built Python + Slurm job-array automation for strong-scaling benchmarks and MPI rank-distribution studies, producing throughput reports that validate cluster performance across node configurations.

experience.role: GPU Research Intern, Advisor: Dr. Huiyang Zhou
bullets: Profiled and optimized a Triton-based LIF spiking-neuron kernel (SpikingJelly) on NVIDIA H100/H200 GPUs, achieving a ~4.45x throughput improvement over the PyTorch baseline
Analyzed kernel efficiency, memory bandwidth, and occupancy with Nsight Compute; iterated on launch configurations and resolved on-cluster profiling permissions to raise GPU utilization
Benchmarked spiking-network inference efficiency across GPU hardware, contributing results toward a Triton kernel publication

deepDive.title: Optimizing a Triton Spiking-Neuron Kernel on H100/H200
deepDive.summary: Profiled and optimized a Triton-based LIF spiking-neuron kernel (SpikingJelly) on NVIDIA H100/H200 GPUs under Dr. Huiyang Zhou, achieving a ~4.45x throughput improvement over the PyTorch baseline.
body: Profiled the SpikingJelly LIF spiking-neuron kernel end-to-end on H100/H200 GPUs, isolating the gaps between the PyTorch baseline and a hand-tuned Triton implementation.

Used Nsight Compute to analyze kernel efficiency, memory bandwidth, and occupancy, iterating on launch configurations and resolving on-cluster profiling permission issues to unblock utilization gains.

Achieved a ~4.45x throughput improvement over the PyTorch baseline and benchmarked spiking-network inference efficiency across GPU hardware, with results contributing toward a Triton kernel publication.

skills.title: Cloud & DevOps
items: Google Cloud (GKE, Cloud Run, VMs, Cloud SQL, LB)
